004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *

010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.microservice.resources;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.IoUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
023
024import java.io.*;
025import java.nio.file.*;
026import java.time.*;
027import java.util.*;
028
029import org.apache.juneau.bean.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.config.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.http.Path;
036import org.apache.juneau.http.classic.response.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.marshall.html.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.beans.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
041
042/**
043 * REST resource that allows access to a file system directory.
044 *
045 * <p>
046 * The root directory is specified in one of two ways:
047 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
048 *    <li>
049 *       Specifying the location via a <l>DirectoryResource.rootDir</l> property.
050 *    <li>
051 *       Overriding the {@link #getRootDir()} method.
052 * </ul>
053 *
054 * <p>
055 * Read/write access control is handled through the following properties:
056 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
057 *    <li>
058 *       <l>DirectoryResource.allowViews</l> - If <jk>true</jk>, allows view and download access to files.
059 *    <li>
060 *       <l>DirectoryResource.allowUploads</l> - If <jk>true</jk>, allows files to be created or overwritten.
061 *    <li>
062 *       <l>DirectoryResource.allowDeletes</l> - If <jk>true</jk>, allows files to be deleted.
063 * </ul>
064 *
065 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
066 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauMicroserviceBasics">juneau-microservice Basics</a>
067 * </ul>
068 *
069 * @serial exclude
070 */
071@Rest(
072   title="File System Explorer",
073   messages="nls/DirectoryResource",
074   allowedMethodParams="*"
075)
076@HtmlDocConfig(
077   navlinks={
078      "up: request:/..",
079      "api: servlet:/api"
080   }
081)
082@HtmlConfig(uriAnchorText="PROPERTY_NAME")
083@SuppressWarnings({
084   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_rootDir)
085   "resource" // Streams returned to servlet container; lifecycle managed by the container
086})
087public class DirectoryResource extends BasicRestServlet {
088
089   /** File action link. */
090   @Response
091   @Schema(description = "File action")
092   public static class Action extends LinkString {
093
094      /**
095       * Constructor.
096       *
097       * @param name Action name.
098       * @param uri Action URI.
099       * @param uriArgs URI format arguments.
100       */
101      public Action(String name, String uri, Object...uriArgs) {
102         super(name, uri, uriArgs);
103      }
104
105      /** {@inheritDoc} */
106      @Override /* Overridden from LinkString */
107      public Action setName(String value) {
108         super.setName(value);
109         return this;
110      }
111
112      /** {@inheritDoc} */
113      @Override /* Overridden from LinkString */
114      public Action setUri(java.net.URI value) {
115         super.setUri(value);
116         return this;
117      }
118
119      /** {@inheritDoc} */
120      @Override /* Overridden from LinkString */
121      public Action setUri(String value) {
122         super.setUri(value);
123         return this;
124      }
125
126      /** {@inheritDoc} */
127      @Override /* Overridden from LinkString */
128      public Action setUri(String value, Object...args) {
129         super.setUri(value, args);
130         return this;
131      }
132   }
133
134   /** File or directory details for REST response. */
135   @Response
136   @Schema(description = "File or directory details")
137   @BeanType(properties = "type,name,size,lastModified,actions,files")
138   public class FileResource {
139      private final File f;
140      private final String path;
141      private final String uri;
142      private final boolean includeChildren;
143
144      /**
145       * Constructor.
146       *
147       * @param f The file or directory.
148       * @param path The path.
149       * @param includeChildren Whether to include child files.
150       */
151      public FileResource(File f, String path, boolean includeChildren) {
152         this.f = f;
153         this.path = path;
154         this.uri = "servlet:/" + (path == null ? "" : path);
155         this.includeChildren = includeChildren;
156      }
157
158      /**
159       * Returns available actions for this file (view, download, delete).
160       *
161       * @return The list of actions.
162       */
163      @Html(format = HtmlFormat.HTML_CDC)
164      public List<Action> getActions() {
165         List<Action> l = list();
166         if (allowViews && f.canRead() && ! f.isDirectory()) {
167            l.add(new Action("view", uri + "?method=VIEW"));
168            l.add(new Action("download", uri + "?method=DOWNLOAD"));
169         }
170         if (allowDeletes && f.canWrite() && ! f.isDirectory())
171            l.add(new Action("delete", uri + "?method=DELETE"));
172         return l;
173      }
174
175      /**
176       * Returns child files when this is a directory and includeChildren is true.
177       *
178       * @return Child files, or <jk>null</jk> when this is a file or includeChildren is false.
179       */
180      @SuppressWarnings({
181         "java:S1168"     // Intentional null when file or !includeChildren.
182      })
183      public Set<FileResource> getFiles() {
184         if (f.isFile() || ! includeChildren)
185            return null;
186         var s = new TreeSet<>(new FileResourceComparator());
187         for (var fc : f.listFiles())
188            s.add(new FileResource(fc, (nn(path) ? (path + '/') : "") + urlEncode(fc.getName()), false));
189         return s;
190      }
191
192      /**
193       * Returns the last modified date of the file.
194       *
195       * @return The last modified date as an {@link Instant}.
196       */
197      public Instant getLastModified() { return Instant.ofEpochMilli(f.lastModified()); }
198
199      /**
200       * Returns the file name as a link.
201       *
202       * @return The name as a link.
203       */
204      public LinkString getName() { return new LinkString(f.getName(), uri); }
205
206      /**
207       * Returns the file size, or number of items for directories.
208       *
209       * @return The size.
210       */
211      public long getSize() { return f.isDirectory() ? f.listFiles().length : f.length(); }
212
213      /**
214       * Returns the type: "dir" or "file".
215       *
216       * @return The type.
217       */
218      public String getType() { return (f.isDirectory() ? "dir" : "file"); }
219   }
220
221   @Response
222   @Schema(type = "string", format = "binary", description = "Contents of file")
223   static class FileContents extends FileInputStream {
224
225      /**
226       * Constructor.
227       *
228       * @param file The file to read.
229       * @throws FileNotFoundException If the file is not found.
230       */
231      public FileContents(File file) throws FileNotFoundException {
232         super(file);
233      }
234   }
235
236   /** Comparator for sorting file resources (directories before files, then by name). */
237   static class FileResourceComparator implements Comparator<FileResource>, Serializable {
238      private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
239
240      /** {@inheritDoc} */
241      @Override /* Overridden from Comparator */
242      public int compare(FileResource o1, FileResource o2) {
243         int c = o1.getType().compareTo(o2.getType());
244         return c != 0 ? c : o1.getName().compareTo(o2.getName());
245      }
246   }
247
248   @Response
249   @Schema(description = "Redirect to root page on success")
250   static class RedirectToRoot extends SeeOtherRoot {}
251
252   private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
253   private static final String PREFIX = "DirectoryResource.";
254
255   /**
256    * Root directory.
257    */
258   public static final String DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_rootDir = PREFIX + "rootDir.s";
259
260   /**
261    * Allow view and downloads on files.
262    */
263   public static final String DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_allowViews = PREFIX + "allowViews.b";
264
265   /**
266    * Allow deletes on files.
267    */
268   public static final String DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_allowDeletes = PREFIX + "allowDeletes.b";
269
270   /**
271    * Allow uploads on files.
272    */
273   public static final String DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_allowUploads = PREFIX + "allowUploads.b";
274
275   private final File rootDir;     // The root directory
276
277   // Properties enabled through servlet init parameters
278   final boolean allowDeletes;
279   final boolean allowUploads;
280   final boolean allowViews;
281
282   /**
283    * Constructor.
284    *
285    * @param c The microservice configuration.
286    */
287   public DirectoryResource(Config c) {
288      rootDir = new File(c.get(DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_rootDir).orElse("."));
289      allowViews = c.get(DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_allowViews).asBoolean().orElse(false);
290      allowDeletes = c.get(DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_allowDeletes).asBoolean().orElse(false);
291      allowUploads = c.get(DIRECTORY_RESOURCE_allowUploads).asBoolean().orElse(false);
292   }
293   /**
294    * Deletes a file on the file system.
295    *
296    * @param path The file path.
297    * @return Redirect to root on success.
298    * @throws MethodNotAllowed If deletes are not enabled.
299    */
300   @RestDelete(
301      path="/*",
302      summary="Delete file",
303      description="Delete a file on the file system."
304   )
305   public RedirectToRoot deleteFile(@Path("/*") String path) throws MethodNotAllowed {
306      deleteFile(getFile(path));
307      return new RedirectToRoot();
308   }
309
310   /**
311    * Downloads the contents of a file.
312    *
313    * @param res The response.
314    * @param path The file path.
315    * @return The file contents stream.
316    * @throws NotFound If the file is not found.
317    * @throws MethodNotAllowed If views are not enabled.
318    */
319   @RestOp(
320      method="DOWNLOAD",
321      path="/*",
322      summary="Download file",
323      description="Download the contents of a file.\nContent-Type is set to 'application/octet-stream'."
324   )
325   public FileContents downloadFile(RestResponse res, @Path("/*") String path) throws NotFound, MethodNotAllowed {
326      if (! allowViews)
327         throw new MethodNotAllowed("DOWNLOAD not enabled");
328
329      res.setContentType("application/octet-stream");
330      try {
331         return new FileContents(getFile(path));
332      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") FileNotFoundException e) {
333         throw new NotFound("File not found");
334      }
335   }
336
337   /**
338    * Returns information about the specified file or directory.
339    *
340    * @param req The request.
341    * @param path The file path.
342    * @return The file or directory details.
343    * @throws NotFound If the file is not found.
344    * @throws Exception If an error occurs.
345    */
346   @RestGet(
347      path="/*",
348      summary="View information on file or directory",
349      description="Returns information about the specified file or directory."
350   )
351   @HtmlDocConfig(
352      nav={"<h5>Folder:  $RA{fullPath}</h5>"}
353   )
354   @SuppressWarnings({
355      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
356   })
357   public FileResource getFile(RestRequest req, @Path("/*") String path) throws NotFound, Exception {
358
359      var dir = getFile(path);
360      req.setAttribute("fullPath", dir.getAbsolutePath());
361
362      return new FileResource(dir, path, true);
363   }
364
365   /**
366    * Adds or overwrites a file on the file system.
367    *
368    * @param is The file contents.
369    * @param path The file path.
370    * @return Redirect to root on success.
371    * @throws InternalServerError If the write fails.
372    */
373   @RestPut(
374      path="/*",
375      summary="Add or replace file",
376      description="Add or overwrite a file on the file system."
377   )
378   public RedirectToRoot updateFile(
379      @Content @Schema(type="string",format="binary") InputStream is,
380      @Path("/*") String path
381   ) throws InternalServerError {
382
383      if (! allowUploads)
384         throw new MethodNotAllowed("PUT not enabled");
385
386      var f = getFileForUpload(path);
387
388      try (var os = new BufferedOutputStream(new FileOutputStream(f))) {
389         pipe(is, os);
390      } catch (IOException e) {
391         throw new InternalServerError(e);
392      }
393
394      return new RedirectToRoot();
395   }
396
397   /**
398    * Views the contents of a file.
399    *
400    * @param res The response.
401    * @param path The file path.
402    * @return The file contents stream.
403    * @throws NotFound If the file is not found.
404    * @throws MethodNotAllowed If views are not enabled.
405    */
406   @RestOp(
407      method="VIEW",
408      path="/*",
409      summary="View contents of file",
410      description="View the contents of a file.\nContent-Type is set to 'text/plain'."
411   )
412   public FileContents viewFile(RestResponse res, @Path("/*") String path) throws NotFound, MethodNotAllowed {
413      if (! allowViews)
414         throw new MethodNotAllowed("VIEW not enabled");
415
416      res.setContentType("text/plain");
417      try {
418         return new FileContents(getFile(path));
419      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") FileNotFoundException e) {
420         throw new NotFound("File not found");
421      }
422   }
423
424   private void deleteFile(File f) {
425      if (! allowDeletes)
426         throw new MethodNotAllowed("DELETE not enabled");
427      if (f.isDirectory()) {
428         var files = f.listFiles();
429         if (nn(files)) {
430            for (var fc : files)
431               deleteFile(fc);
432         }
433      }
434      try {
435         Files.delete(f.toPath());
436      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") IOException e) {
437         throw new Forbidden("Could not delete file {0}", f.getAbsolutePath());
438      }
439   }
440
441   /**
442    * Resolves a request path against {@link #rootDir} with a canonical-path boundary check
443    * so {@code ..} segments, absolute paths, and symlink-out cannot escape the configured root.
444    *
445    * <p>
446    * The check is intentionally applied at this single funnel because every public operation on
447    * this resource (view, download, delete, upload, info) resolves the user-supplied path through
448    * this method. Delegates to {@link FileUtils#resolveSafely(File, String)} — the canonical
449    * shared implementation; the caller-layer mapping is: boundary violation → 403 (Forbidden),
450    * non-existent target → 404 (NotFound).
451    */
452   private File getFile(String path) throws NotFound {
453      try {
454         return FileUtils.resolveSafely(rootDir, path)
455            .orElseThrow(() -> new NotFound("File not found."));
456      } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
457         throw new Forbidden(e.getMessage());
458      }
459   }
460
461   /**
462    * Upload-path variant of {@link #getFile(String)} that allows the target file to not yet exist
463    * (so {@code PUT /new-file.txt} can create a new file) while still enforcing the boundary check
464    * by resolving the parent directory through {@link FileUtils#resolveSafely(File, String)}.
465    *
466    * <p>
467    * If the target itself exists, returns it directly (overwrite path). Otherwise, resolves the
468    * parent directory (which must exist and be inside the root), then appends the requested
469    * filename. The parent's existence requirement prevents implicit directory creation via PUT.
470    */
471   private File getFileForUpload(String path) throws NotFound {
472      try {
473         var existing = FileUtils.resolveSafely(rootDir, path);
474         if (existing.isPresent())
475            return existing.get();
476         var parentPath = "";
477         var name = path == null ? "" : path;
478         var slash = name.lastIndexOf('/');
479         if (slash >= 0) {
480            parentPath = name.substring(0, slash);
481            name = name.substring(slash + 1);
482         }
483         if (name.isEmpty())
484            throw new NotFound("File not found.");
485         var parent = FileUtils.resolveSafely(rootDir, parentPath)
486            .orElseThrow(() -> new NotFound("File not found."));
487         return new File(parent, name);
488      } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
489         throw new Forbidden(e.getMessage());
490      }
491   }
492
493   /**
494    * Returns the root directory.
495    *
496    * @return The root directory.
497    */
498   protected File getRootDir() { return rootDir; }
499}
